 {"id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","title":"G9.5: publish the final overlay scorecard and closeout decision record","description":"Publish the final overlay scorecard and closeout decision record once the evidence, interventions, and governance updates settle.

This should leave the graph in a state where a future agent can tell what changed, what remains open, and what to do next without reopening old debate.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:04.212302540Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-24T20:43:45.148371789Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-g","evidence","overlay-2026-03-23","report","scorecard","track-g","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.8.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:40:09.295220391Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:32.845825051Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:27:04.212302540Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:30.088207501Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:30.759012032Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:31.469362339Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:31:32.127982733Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-hjkbr.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T20:43:45.147194344Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2581,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","author":"codex","text":"## Suggested acceptance shape

The closeout here should point future users to the runnable gate entrypoints, the final evidence packs, the approved advanced frontier, and any still-red regimes. A strong result should still be humble enough to say what remains bad or uncertain.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:47:15Z"},{"id":2594,"issue_id":"bd-db300.7.9.5","author":"codex","text":"## Addendum: explicit final verdict semantics

The final scorecard should state, in plain terms, whether each critical benchmark regime cleared both parity and the program's healthy-margin standard. It should not force future users to infer the answer from geomeans or prose.

At minimum the closeout should call out:
- which critical c1 cells are above or below `1.0x`,
- which persistent `2t/4t/8t/16t` regimes are above or below `1.0x`,
- which cells cleared the healthy-margin threshold versus merely scraping above parity,
- what remains red, uncertain, or rolled back.
","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:56:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.8","title":"Track H: single-writer containment and honest comparison-mode cleanup","description":"## Goal
This track isolates and repairs the extra pain in forced single-writer mode without ever making it the product default. Single-writer mode is still important as a comparison path and fallback, but current c4/c8 numbers show that it is suffering from avoidable overhead on top of unavoidable serialization. Cleaning that up makes comparisons fairer and prevents shared fixes from accidentally helping only MVCC.

## Acceptance Criteria
- There is an evidence-backed explanation for why single-writer mode is still slow.
- Avoidable single-writer-only overhead is reduced.
- The final scorecard reports the mode honestly without confusing it for the project's main value proposition.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","design":"- Separate unavoidable serialization from self-inflicted waiting and duplicate work.
- Reuse the same hot-path optimizations wherever possible so improvements benefit both modes.
- Keep the purpose of the mode explicit: comparison/fallback, not the project's default operating model.","acceptance_criteria":"- There is an evidence-backed explanation for why single-writer mode is still slow.
- Avoidable single-writer-only overhead is reduced.
- The final scorecard reports the mode honestly without confusing it for the project's main value proposition.
- This bead set explicitly includes comprehensive unit-test, end-to-end, and structured-logging obligations rather than treating verification as implied follow-up work.","notes":"Current corrected averages show the scale of the problem: c4 mixed_read_write SQLite 2769.5 vs MVCC 424.2 vs single-writer 130.9; c8 mixed_read_write SQLite 965.3 vs MVCC 620.1 vs single-writer 80.8. Some of this is inherent serialization, but not all of it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:47:39.567907034Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:17.975105757Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","busy","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","locking","perf","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2263,"issue_id":"bd-db300.8","author":"codex","text":"Single-writer mode exists to provide an honest reference path and fallback, not to redefine the product. The purpose of this track is to strip away avoidable overhead so comparisons against SQLite are real, while preserving the non-negotiable rule that concurrent-writer mode remains the default and the point of the project.","created_at":"2026-03-10T04:48:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.8.1","title":"H1: profile the slowdown unique to forced single-writer mode","description":"## Goal
Profile the extra slowdown unique to forced single-writer mode under the hot cells and placement profiles that matter so the project can separate inherent serialization cost from self-inflicted overhead and topology-hostile waiting. Right now the mode is so much worse than MVCC that it clearly deserves a targeted breakdown instead of hand-waving.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The dominant single-writer-only costs are identified with evidence on the same canonical hot cells and placement profiles used for SQLite and MVCC comparison.
2. The report distinguishes unavoidable serialization from avoidable waits, wake storms, cache-line ownership churn, or duplicate work.
3. The output includes a side-by-side artifact pack for the matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs so H2 can target only the truly single-writer-specific pain.
4. The findings directly motivate H2 and H3.
5. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The dominant single-writer-only costs are identified with evidence on the same canonical hot cells and placement profiles used for SQLite and MVCC comparison.
2. The report distinguishes unavoidable serialization from avoidable waits, wake storms, cache-line ownership churn, or duplicate work.
3. The output includes a side-by-side artifact pack for the matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer runs so H2 can target only the truly single-writer-specific pain.
4. The findings directly motivate H2 and H3.
5. The scripts, harness changes, or reports in this bead emit structured logs and artifact manifests rich enough to reproduce results without reconstructing the workflow by hand.","notes":"This bead should use the same real Beads fixtures, hot workloads, and placement profiles as the MVCC investigation so the comparison is honest.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:17:30.309793043Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:18.182478454Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","busy","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","perf","profiling","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","title":"H1.1: collect matched artifact packs across modes and placement profiles","description":"## Goal
collect matched artifact packs across modes and placement profiles

## Checklist
1. Collect matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs on the same hot cells and placement profiles.
2. Keep the packs mechanically comparable.
3. Tie them back to canonical matrix rows and provenance.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Collect matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs on the same hot cells and placement profiles.
2. Keep the packs mechanically comparable.
3. Tie them back to canonical matrix rows and provenance.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Matched SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer artifact packs are collected on the same canonical rows and placement profiles, with identical row identity and provenance fields.
2. The packs are mechanically comparable without hand-normalization and include enough evidence to isolate what slowdown is unique to forced single-writer mode versus shared baseline tax.
3. Artifact locations, bundle names, and rerun entrypoints follow the canonical naming/provenance contract so future operators can reproduce and compare them quickly.
4. The resulting pack is strong enough to support later single-writer diagnosis and honest scorecard reporting, not just ad hoc debugging.","notes":"Matched-pack obligations:
- collect side-by-side SQLite, MVCC, and forced single-writer packs for the same hot rows, profiles, binaries, and run identity fields
- require the bundle to contain enough counters/logs/artifacts to separate unavoidable serialization from extra self-inflicted overhead in single-writer mode
- align naming, retention, and provenance with A2.4 so later diagnosis and reporting do not depend on manual artifact archaeology
- optimize for users by giving them one directly comparable evidence pack instead of three separately interpreted runs","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:28:43.854715489Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:39:10.939803208Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-h","evidence","legacy","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:25:32.763073370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.8.2","title":"H2: remove single-writer-only wait amplification and duplicate serialized work","description":"## Goal
Remove single-writer-specific wait amplification and duplicated serialized work that makes the fallback or reference path far worse than unavoidable serialization alone would suggest. The target is not to make single-writer mode the star, but to stop it from distorting comparisons through topology-blind baton passing, generic waits, or duplicated work inside the serialized region.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. At least one avoidable single-writer-only source of waiting, wake amplification, or duplicate work is removed.
2. Hot-cell benchmarks show the mode improved on its own terms across the same placement profiles used in H1.
3. The cleanup does not change the project's MVCC-by-default behavior.
4. Targeted unit tests are added or refreshed for the exact invariants and regressions touched by this bead.
5. Where behavior crosses subsystem boundaries, canonical-matrix end-to-end validation with detailed structured logging is added or updated alongside the change.","notes":"This bead is likely to intersect both retry policy and commit-path scope. It should lean on B1's lock map rather than rediscovering the same synchronization topology from scratch, and it should be careful not to turn single-writer cleanup into a justification for weakening MVCC.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:18:06.438428589Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:51:18.376498375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["busy","critical-path","db300-program","db300-track-h","legacy","locking","perf","sqlite","track-h"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:03Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
